expectations.text: Bug identified: adams_id should NOT be used — script incorrectly specifies adams_id = 1 for all parts/joints/forces
expectations.evidence: Bug 4 explicitly flags 'adams_id = 3' as violating Core Rule 3 and removes it from the corrected script.

expectations.text: Bug identified: Abbreviated commands used — e.g., 'part mod' instead of 'part modify', 'sim trans' instead of 'simulation single_run transient'
expectations.evidence: Bug 1 flags 'model cre' as an abbreviation of 'model create'; Bug 2 flags 'part cre rig nam' as abbreviations of 'part create rigid_body name_and_position'. Both are corrected in the fixed script.

expectations.text: Bug identified: center_of_mass_marker argument passed in part modify rigid_body mass_properties (it should not be)
expectations.evidence: Bug 5 explicitly flags 'center_of_mass_marker = .pendulum.link.cm' and explains Adams auto-creates the .cm marker, removing it from the corrected script.

expectations.text: Bug identified: Marker names use incorrect format — e.g. 'ground_j' instead of '.model.ground.ground_j' (missing full dot-path hierarchy)
expectations.evidence: Bug 6 and Bug 7 both flag missing leading dots on 'pendulum.link.pin_mkr', correcting to '.pendulum.link.pin_mkr' in both marker_name and i_marker_name arguments.

expectations.text: Bug identified: Wrong joint type used — fixed joint on a point mass (Adams only allows spherical or primitive joints on point masses)
expectations.evidence: No mention of a fixed joint on a point mass anywhere in the response. The response only analyzes a revolute joint setup.

expectations.text: Bug identified: Wrong spring-damper keyword — 'spring_damper' used instead of 'translational_spring_damper'
expectations.evidence: No mention of spring_damper or translational_spring_damper anywhere in the response.
